title: First-Order Signature
statement:
A \textbf{first-order signature} $\mathcal{L}$ consists of:
 - \textbf{Constant symbols} $c_1, c_2, \ldots$ — naming distinguished
 elements,
- \textbf{Function symbols} $f_1, f_2, \ldots$, each equipped with a
 specified \textbf{arity} $n \in \mathbb{N}$ — naming $n$-ary
 operations,
- \textbf{Relation symbols} $R_1, R_2, \ldots$ (optional), each with a
 specified arity — naming predicates.
